data.name: Title
data.value: Keep users, clients, and permissions under control.

data.name: Lead
data.value: Use this overview to spot access changes quickly, review connected apps, and jump into the admin pages that need attention.

data.name: TotalUsersLead
data.value: Accounts that can currently sign in to this identity workspace.

data.name: AdminCoverageLead
data.value: {0} of {1} accounts currently hold admin access.

data.name: TotalClientsLead
data.value: Applications and service integrations registered with this sign-in platform.

data.name: ConfiguredScopesLead
data.value: {0} permission mappings currently define what connected apps are allowed to request.

data.name: ClientMixLead
data.value: Compare interactive apps, machine clients, and permission mappings to understand how this workspace is wired together.

data.name: QuickActionsTitle
data.value: Go straight to the core admin pages

data.name: ManageUsersLead
data.value: Review accounts, adjust admin access, and clean up stale user records.

data.name: ManageClientsLead
data.value: Inspect app registrations, redirect URIs, secrets, and granted scopes.

data.name: RegisterClientLead
data.value: Create a new client record for an internal app or service integration.

data.name: GuidanceTitle
data.value: Before you publish or change an integration

data.name: GuidanceOne
data.value: Make sure the client type matches the real sign-in flow, otherwise login and token refresh behavior will drift from the product design.

data.name: GuidanceTwo
data.value: Grant only the scopes the integration actually needs so later audits and incident response stay manageable.

data.name: GuidanceThree
data.value: Double-check redirect URIs, permissions, and environment-specific settings before release so the integration works the same in production as it did in testing.

data.name: AccessLimitedTitle
data.value: You don't have access to this overview.

data.name: AccessLimitedLead
data.value: Sign in with an account that can open the admin workspace to view the latest summary.
